sentences.sentence: Why does music make us feel?
sentences.translation: 为什么音乐会让我们产生感受？
difficult_points: make us feel 是使役结构，宾语 us 后接不带 to 的动词原形 feel。
Why 引出全文核心问题，后文围绕音乐为何能激发情绪展开解释。

sentences.sentence: On the one hand, music is a purely abstract art form, devoid of language or explicit ideas.
sentences.translation: 一方面，音乐是一种纯粹抽象的艺术形式，不含语言或明确的思想。

structure.text: devoid of language or explicit ideas

difficult_points: On the one hand 标记让步式铺垫，暗示后文会出现相反或补充观点。
devoid of... 是后置形容词短语，补充说明 music 这种艺术形式缺少语言和明确观念。

sentences.sentence: And yet, even though music says little, it still manages to touch us deeply.
sentences.translation: 然而，即使音乐几乎不表达具体内容，它仍然能深深打动我们。

structure.text: it still manages to touch us deeply

difficult_points: And yet 与 even though 共同制造转折，突出“抽象却能打动人”的矛盾。
it 指代前面的 music，manages to 强调虽看似困难但仍然做到。

sentences.sentence: When listening to our favourite songs, our body betrays all the symptoms of emotional arousal.
sentences.translation: 当我们听最喜欢的歌曲时，身体会显露出情绪唤起的全部症状。

structure.text: When listening to our favourite songs
all the symptoms of emotional arousal

difficult_points: When listening... 是省略结构，完整含义相当于 when we are listening。
betrays 在此不是“背叛”，而是“泄露、显露”身体反应。

sentences.sentence: The pupils in our eyes dilate, our pulse and blood pressure rise, the electrical conductance of our skin is lowered, and the cerebellum, a brain region associated with bodily movement, becomes strangely active.
sentences.translation: 我们的瞳孔会放大，脉搏和血压会上升，皮肤的电导率会降低，而小脑——一个与身体运动相关的大脑区域——会变得异常活跃。

structure.text: The pupils in our eyes dilate
our pulse and blood pressure rise
the electrical conductance of our skin is lowered
the cerebellum, a brain region associated with bodily movement, becomes strangely active
a brain region associated with bodily movement

difficult_points: 本句由多个并列分句构成，连续列举听音乐时身体和神经系统的反应。
is lowered 是被动语态，说明皮肤电导率这一指标被降低。
a brain region associated with bodily movement 是 cerebellum 的同位语，解释“小脑”的功能关联。

sentences.sentence: Blood is even redirected to the muscles in our legs.
sentences.translation: 血液甚至会被重新导向我们腿部的肌肉。

structure.text: to the muscles in our legs

difficult_points: is redirected 是被动语态，表示血液流向被身体自动调整。
even 强调这种生理反应程度之深，连腿部肌肉供血也会变化。

sentences.sentence: In other words, sound stirs us at our biological roots.
sentences.translation: 换句话说，声音在我们的生物本能层面触动了我们。
difficult_points: In other words 对前面多种生理反应进行概括总结。
at our biological roots 是比喻性表达，指音乐影响的是人类深层生理机制。

sentences.sentence: A recent paper in Nature Neuroscience by a research team in Montreal, Canada, marks an important step in revealing the precise underpinnings of 'the potent pleasurable stimulus' that is music.
sentences.translation: 加拿大蒙特利尔一个研究团队在《自然神经科学》上发表的近期论文，是揭示音乐这一“强有力的愉悦刺激”背后精确机制的重要一步。

structure.text: A recent paper in Nature Neuroscience by a research team in Montreal, Canada
in revealing the precise underpinnings of 'the potent pleasurable stimulus' that is music

difficult_points: 主语很长，核心是 A recent paper，后面的 in Nature Neuroscience 和 by a research team 都是修饰成分。
in revealing... 说明这篇论文“重要一步”的具体方向。
that is music 修饰引号中的 stimulus，说明这种强烈愉悦刺激就是音乐。

sentences.sentence: Although the study involves plenty of fancy technology, including functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) and ligand-based positron emission tomography (PET) scanning, the experiment itself was rather straightforward.
sentences.translation: 尽管这项研究使用了大量先进技术，包括功能性磁共振成像（fMRI）和基于配体的正电子发射断层扫描（PET），但实验本身相当直接。

structure.text: Although the study involves plenty of fancy technology
including functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) and ligand-based positron emission tomography (PET) scanning
the experiment itself was rather straightforward

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，强调技术复杂但实验设计并不复杂。
including... 是补充说明 technology 的非谓语结构，列举两种脑成像技术。
itself 强调“实验本身”，与前面的 fancy technology 形成对照。

sentences.sentence: After screening 217 individuals who responded to advertisements requesting people who experience 'chills' to instrumental music, the scientists narrowed down the subject pool to ten.
sentences.translation: 在筛选了217名响应广告的人之后，科学家把受试者范围缩小到10人；这些广告寻找的是听器乐会产生“寒战”体验的人。

structure.text: After screening 217 individuals who responded to advertisements requesting people who experience 'chills' to instrumental music
who responded to advertisements requesting people who experience 'chills' to instrumental music
requesting people who experience 'chills' to instrumental music
the scientists narrowed down the subject pool to ten

difficult_points: After screening... 是句首时间/步骤背景，主句动作是 narrowed down。
who responded... 修饰 individuals，requesting... 修饰 advertisements，内部又有 who experience... 修饰 people。
chills 在此指听音乐时产生的生理性战栗或鸡皮疙瘩，不是普通寒冷。

sentences.sentence: They then asked the subjects to bring in their playlist of favourite songs—virtually every genre was represented, from techno to tango—and played them the music while their brain activity was monitored.
sentences.translation: 随后他们要求受试者带来自己最喜欢歌曲的播放列表——几乎每种音乐类型都有，从电子舞曲到探戈——并在监测其脑活动的同时播放这些音乐。

structure.text: They then asked the subjects to bring in their playlist of favourite songs
to bring in their playlist of favourite songs
while their brain activity was monitored

difficult_points: 破折号中间的内容是插入说明，解释播放列表覆盖的音乐类型很广。
asked...to bring in... 与 played... 共享主语 They，构成连续实验步骤。
while their brain activity was monitored 是时间状语从句，使用被动语态说明脑活动被监测。

sentences.sentence: Because the scientists were combining methodologies (PET and fMRI), they were able to obtain an impressively exact and detailed portrait of music in the brain.
sentences.translation: 由于科学家结合了两种方法（PET 和 fMRI），他们能够获得一幅极其精确而详细的音乐在大脑中作用的图像。

structure.text: Because the scientists were combining methodologies (PET and fMRI)
they were able to obtain an impressively exact and detailed portrait of music in the brain
to obtain an impressively exact and detailed portrait of music in the brain

difficult_points: Because 引导原因状语从句，说明为何能得到精确的大脑图像。
were able to 强调研究方法带来的能力或结果，而不仅是一般可能性。
portrait 在科学语境中是比喻，指对音乐在大脑中表现的详细描绘。

sentences.sentence: The first thing they discovered is that music triggers the production of dopamine—a chemical with a key role in setting people's moods—by the neurons (nerve cells) in both the dorsal and ventral regions of the brain.
sentences.translation: 他们发现的第一件事是，音乐会促使大脑背侧和腹侧区域的神经元（神经细胞）产生多巴胺；多巴胺是一种在调节人们情绪中起关键作用的化学物质。

structure.text: that music triggers the production of dopamine—a chemical with a key role in setting people's moods—by the neurons (nerve cells) in both the dorsal and ventral regions of the brain
a chemical with a key role in setting people's moods
by the neurons (nerve cells) in both the dorsal and ventral regions of the brain

difficult_points: The first thing they discovered 中 they discovered 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 thing。
that 引导表语从句，解释“第一个发现”的具体内容。
破折号中的 a chemical... 是 dopamine 的同位语，补充其调节情绪的作用。
by the neurons... 说明产生 dopamine 的主体是神经元。

sentences.sentence: As these two regions have long been linked with the experience of pleasure, this finding isn't particularly surprising.
sentences.translation: 由于这两个区域长期以来一直与愉悦体验相关，这一发现并不特别令人意外。

structure.text: As these two regions have long been linked with the experience of pleasure

difficult_points: As 在此表示原因，相当于 because。
have long been linked with 是现在完成时的被动结构，表示这种关联早已被认识到。
this finding 指上一句音乐触发多巴胺产生的发现。

sentences.sentence: What is rather more significant is the finding that the dopamine neurons in the caudate—a region of the brain involved in learning stimulus-response associations, and in anticipating food and other 'reward' stimuli—were at their most active around 15 seconds before the participants' favourite moments in the music.
sentences.translation: 更重要的是这样一个发现：尾状核中的多巴胺神经元在参与者最喜欢的音乐片段到来前约15秒最为活跃；尾状核是大脑中参与学习刺激—反应关联以及预期食物和其他“奖励”刺激的区域。

structure.text: that the dopamine neurons in the caudate—a region of the brain involved in learning stimulus-response associations, and in anticipating food and other 'reward' stimuli—were at their most active around 15 seconds before the participants' favourite moments in the music
a region of the brain involved in learning stimulus-response associations, and in anticipating food and other 'reward' stimuli
involved in learning stimulus-response associations, and in anticipating food and other 'reward' stimuli
around 15 seconds before the participants' favourite moments in the music

difficult_points: What is rather more significant 是名词性从句作主语，后面的 is the finding 是主句谓语和表语。
that 引导同位语从句，解释 finding 的具体内容。
破折号中间的 a region... 是 caudate 的同位语，说明该脑区的功能。
before the participants' favourite moments... 是时间关系，关键发现是神经元在高潮到来前已活跃。

sentences.sentence: The researchers call this the 'anticipatory phase' and argue that the purpose of this activity is to help us predict the arrival of our favourite part.
sentences.translation: 研究人员把这称为“预期阶段”，并认为这种活动的目的在于帮助我们预测最喜欢的部分即将到来。

structure.text: that the purpose of this activity is to help us predict the arrival of our favourite part
to help us predict the arrival of our favourite part
predict the arrival of our favourite part

difficult_points: call this the 'anticipatory phase' 是“call + 宾语 + 宾补”结构。
argue 后接 that 宾语从句，说明研究人员对这种活动目的的解释。
help us predict 中 predict 是省略 to 的动词原形。

sentences.sentence: The question, of course, is what all these dopamine neurons are up to.
sentences.translation: 当然，问题在于所有这些多巴胺神经元到底在做什么。

structure.text: what all these dopamine neurons are up to

difficult_points: what 引导表语从句，放在 is 后说明 question 的具体内容。
are up to 是口语化短语，意为“在做什么、意图是什么”。

sentences.sentence: Why are they so active in the period preceding the acoustic climax?
sentences.translation: 为什么它们在声学高潮到来之前的阶段会如此活跃？

structure.text: in the period preceding the acoustic climax

difficult_points: they 指代上一句中的 dopamine neurons。
preceding the acoustic climax 是现在分词短语，后置修饰 the period。
acoustic climax 指音乐声音结构上的高潮，不一定是情绪表达上的结论。

sentences.sentence: After all, we typically associate surges of dopamine with pleasure, with the processing of actual rewards.
sentences.translation: 毕竟，我们通常把多巴胺的激增与愉悦、与实际奖励的处理联系在一起。

structure.text: with the processing of actual rewards

difficult_points: After all 用来引出常识性理由，解释为什么前一句问题值得追问。
associate A with B 结构中 A 是 surges of dopamine，B 是 pleasure 和 the processing of actual rewards。
两个 with 短语并列，都是 associate 的搭配成分。

sentences.sentence: And yet, this cluster of cells is most active when the 'chills' have yet to arrive, when the melodic pattern is still unresolved.
sentences.translation: 然而，这一簇细胞最活跃的时候，恰恰是“寒战”尚未到来、旋律模式仍未解决的时候。

structure.text: when the 'chills' have yet to arrive
when the melodic pattern is still unresolved

difficult_points: And yet 表示与前面“多巴胺通常和实际奖励相关”的认知形成反差。
have yet to arrive 表示“尚未到来”，强调奖励体验还未真正发生。
两个 when 从句并列，说明细胞最活跃的时间状态。

sentences.sentence: One way to answer the question is to look at the music and not the neurons.
sentences.translation: 回答这个问题的一种方式，是去看音乐本身，而不是只看神经元。

structure.text: One way to answer the question
to look at the music and not the neurons

difficult_points: 主语 One way 后接 to answer the question 作后置修饰，说明是哪种方式。
表语是不定式 to look at...，说明这种方式的具体做法。
and not the neurons 构成对比，提示解释角度从神经元转向音乐结构。

sentences.sentence: While music can often seem (at least to the outsider) like a labyrinth of intricate patterns, it turns out that the most important part of every song or symphony is when the patterns break down, when the sound becomes unpredictable.
sentences.translation: 虽然音乐常常（至少在外行看来）像一个由复杂模式构成的迷宫，但事实证明，每首歌或交响曲最重要的部分，是模式瓦解、声音变得不可预测的时候。

structure.text: While music can often seem (at least to the outsider) like a labyrinth of intricate patterns
like a labyrinth of intricate patterns
that the most important part of every song or symphony is when the patterns break down, when the sound becomes unpredictable

difficult_points: While 引导让步状语从句，先承认音乐看似复杂。
it turns out that... 是固定句式，that 从句才是“事实证明”的具体内容。
两个 when 从句并列作 is 的表语，说明最重要部分发生在何时。
(at least to the outsider) 是插入语，限定“像迷宫”只是外行人的观感。

sentences.sentence: If the music is too obvious, it is annoyingly boring, like an alarm clock.
sentences.translation: 如果音乐太明显、太可预测，它就会像闹钟一样令人厌烦地无聊。

structure.text: If the music is too obvious

difficult_points: If 从句提出条件：音乐模式过于明显。
it 指代 the music，主句说明这种条件下的结果。
like an alarm clock 用类比说明高度可预测的声音会令人厌烦。

sentences.sentence: Numerous studies, after all, have demonstrated that dopamine neurons quickly adapt to predictable rewards.
sentences.translation: 毕竟，许多研究已经证明，多巴胺神经元会迅速适应可预测的奖励。

structure.text: that dopamine neurons quickly adapt to predictable rewards

difficult_points: after all 是插入语，为上一句关于“无聊”的判断提供研究依据。
have demonstrated 是现在完成时，强调已有研究结论与现在讨论相关。
that 引导宾语从句，作 demonstrated 的内容。

sentences.sentence: If we know what's going to happen next, then we don't get excited.
sentences.translation: 如果我们知道接下来会发生什么，我们就不会兴奋。

structure.text: If we know what's going to happen next

difficult_points: If 引导条件状语从句，then 引出对应结果。
what's going to happen next 是 know 的宾语从句。
get excited 表示进入兴奋状态，get 作系动词。

sentences.sentence: This is why composers often introduce a key note in the beginning of a song, spend most of the rest of the piece in the studious avoidance of the pattern, and then finally repeat it only at the end.
sentences.translation: 这就是为什么作曲家常常在一首歌开头引入一个关键音符，然后在作品剩余的大部分时间里刻意避免这个模式，最后只在结尾才重复它。

structure.text: why composers often introduce a key note in the beginning of a song, spend most of the rest of the piece in the studious avoidance of the pattern, and then finally repeat it only at the end
composers often introduce a key note in the beginning of a song
spend most of the rest of the piece in the studious avoidance of the pattern
then finally repeat it only at the end

difficult_points: This is why... 引出由前文神经元适应可预测奖励得出的解释。
why 引导表语从句，内部有 introduce、spend、repeat 三个并列动作。
it 指代前面的 a key note 或相关音乐模式。
studious avoidance 表示“刻意回避”，说明作曲家有意延迟满足听众预期。

sentences.sentence: The longer we are denied the pattern we expect, the greater the emotional release when the pattern returns, safe and sound.
sentences.translation: 我们期待的模式被延迟得越久，当这个模式安然回归时，情感释放就越强烈。

structure.text: The longer we are denied the pattern we expect
we are denied the pattern we expect

difficult_points: The longer..., the greater... 是“越……越……”比较结构。
we are denied the pattern 是被动结构，表示我们期待的模式被暂时剥夺或延迟。
we expect 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the pattern。
safe and sound 后置补充说明 pattern returns 的状态。

sentences.sentence: To demonstrate this psychological principle, the musicologist Leonard Meyer, in his classic book Emotion and Meaning in Music (1956), analysed the 5th movement of Beethoven's String Quartet in C-sharp minor, Op. 131.
sentences.translation: 为了证明这一心理原则，音乐学家伦纳德·迈耶在其经典著作《音乐中的情感与意义》（1956）中分析了贝多芬《升C小调弦乐四重奏》作品131的第五乐章。

structure.text: in his classic book Emotion and Meaning in Music (1956)
the 5th movement of Beethoven's String Quartet in C-sharp minor, Op. 131

difficult_points: To demonstrate... 是目的状语，说明引用 Meyer 分析的功能。
in his classic book... 是插入的介词短语，交代分析出现的出处。
长宾语包含作品名、调性和作品编号，需要整体理解为被分析的音乐作品。

sentences.sentence: Meyer wanted to show how music is defined by its flirtation with—but not submission to—our expectations of order.
sentences.translation: 迈耶想要展示，音乐是如何通过与我们对秩序的期待“调情”但并不屈从于这种期待来界定自身的。

structure.text: how music is defined by its flirtation with—but not submission to—our expectations of order
its flirtation with—but not submission to—our expectations of order

difficult_points: how 引导宾语从句，作 show 的宾语。
is defined by 是被动语态，说明音乐的特征由某种关系决定。
with—but not submission to— 是破折号插入的对比结构，强调音乐接近期待但不完全满足期待。

sentences.sentence: Meyer dissected 50 measures (bars) of the masterpiece, showing how Beethoven begins with the clear statement of a rhythmic and harmonic pattern and then, in an ingenious tonal dance, carefully holds off repeating it.
sentences.translation: 迈耶剖析了这部杰作的50个小节，展示了贝多芬如何先清楚呈现一个节奏与和声模式，然后又以巧妙的音调舞步小心地延迟重复它。

structure.text: showing how Beethoven begins with the clear statement of a rhythmic and harmonic pattern and then, in an ingenious tonal dance, carefully holds off repeating it
how Beethoven begins with the clear statement of a rhythmic and harmonic pattern and then, in an ingenious tonal dance, carefully holds off repeating it

difficult_points: showing... 是现在分词短语，说明 dissected 这一分析动作揭示了什么。
how 引导宾语从句，作 showing 的宾语。
begins with... and then... holds off... 是 Beethoven 在从句中的两个连续动作。
it 指代前面的 a rhythmic and harmonic pattern。

sentences.sentence: What Beethoven does instead is suggest variations of the pattern.
sentences.translation: 贝多芬转而做的是暗示这一模式的各种变体。
difficult_points: What Beethoven does instead 是名词性从句作主语。
is 后省略了 to，表语用动词原形 suggest 表示具体行为。
instead 表明贝多芬没有直接重复该模式，而采取替代做法。

sentences.sentence: He wants to preserve an element of uncertainty in his music, making our brains beg for the one chord he refuses to give us.
sentences.translation: 他想在音乐中保留一种不确定性，让我们的大脑渴求那个他拒绝给予我们的和弦。

structure.text: an element of uncertainty in his music
making our brains beg for the one chord he refuses to give us
our brains beg for the one chord he refuses to give us

difficult_points: making... 是结果状语，说明保留不确定性带来的效果。
make our brains beg for... 是使役结构，beg 使用动词原形。
he refuses to give us 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the one chord。

sentences.sentence: Beethoven saves that chord for the end.
sentences.translation: 贝多芬把那个和弦留到最后。
difficult_points: that chord 指上一句中听众期待却迟迟得不到的 one chord。
save...for... 表示有意把某物保留到某个时刻，呼应延迟满足预期的主题。

sentences.sentence: According to Meyer, it is the suspenseful tension of music, arising out of our unfulfilled expectations, that is the source of the music's feeling.
sentences.translation: 根据迈耶的观点，音乐中源自我们未被满足的期待的悬念张力，正是音乐情感的来源。

structure.text: it is the suspenseful tension of music, arising out of our unfulfilled expectations, that is the source of the music's feeling
arising out of our unfulfilled expectations
that is the source of the music's feeling

difficult_points: it is...that... 是强调句，突出 the suspenseful tension of music 才是音乐情感来源。
arising out of... 是现在分词短语，后置解释 tension 的来源。
According to Meyer 表明这是 Meyer 的理论观点。

sentences.sentence: While earlier theories of music focused on the way a sound can refer to the real world of images and experiences—its 'connotative' meaning—Meyer argued that the emotions we find in music come from the unfolding events of the music itself.
sentences.translation: 早期音乐理论关注声音如何指向由图像和经历构成的现实世界——也就是它的“内涵”意义——而迈耶则认为，我们在音乐中感受到的情绪来自音乐本身展开的事件。

structure.text: While earlier theories of music focused on the way a sound can refer to the real world of images and experiences—its 'connotative' meaning
a sound can refer to the real world of images and experiences
that the emotions we find in music come from the unfolding events of the music itself

difficult_points: While 在此表示对比，而不是单纯时间关系。
破折号中的 its 'connotative' meaning 是对前面 refer to the real world... 的同位解释。
that 引导 argued 的宾语从句，说明 Meyer 与早期理论不同的观点。
we find in music 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the emotions。

sentences.sentence: This 'embodied meaning' arises from the patterns the symphony invokes and then ignores.
sentences.translation: 这种“具身意义”来自交响曲所唤起、随后又忽略的那些模式。

structure.text: the patterns the symphony invokes and then ignores
the symphony invokes and then ignores

difficult_points: This 'embodied meaning' 指上一句 Meyer 所说来自音乐自身展开过程的意义。
the symphony invokes and then ignores 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 the patterns。
invokes and then ignores 形成先唤起再搁置的动作链，呼应延迟满足期待。

sentences.sentence: It is this uncertainty that triggers the surge of dopamine in the caudate, as we struggle to figure out what will happen next.
sentences.translation: 正是这种不确定性触发了尾状核中多巴胺的激增，因为我们正在努力弄清接下来会发生什么。

structure.text: It is this uncertainty that triggers the surge of dopamine in the caudate
triggers the surge of dopamine in the caudate
as we struggle to figure out what will happen next
to figure out what will happen next

difficult_points: It is...that... 是强调句，强调触发多巴胺激增的是 this uncertainty。
as 引导原因或伴随状语从句，说明大脑努力预测时产生这一反应。
what will happen next 是 figure out 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: We can predict some of the notes, but we can't predict them all, and that is what keeps us listening, waiting expectantly for our reward, for the pattern to be completed.
sentences.translation: 我们能预测一些音符，但无法预测全部；而这正是让我们持续聆听、满怀期待地等待奖励、等待模式完成的原因。

structure.text: We can predict some of the notes
but we can't predict them all
and that is what keeps us listening
for the pattern to be completed

difficult_points: but 和 and 连接三个分句，形成“能预测部分—不能预测全部—因此继续听”的逻辑链。
that 指代前面“不能预测全部音符”这一事实。
what keeps us listening 是表语从句，说明保持聆听的原因。
for the pattern to be completed 是带逻辑主语的非谓语结构，表示等待模式被完成。

expressions.text: for the pattern to be completed
